meta.note: Actor-based mutations for OWNER role - Provider and Department management

product_overview: The Owner Management API enables organization owners to manage providers and departments within their organizations. This API uses an actor-based architecture where all mutations (POST, DELETE) are organized by actor (/api/owner/*). Owners can create providers with user accounts, create provider records for existing users, delete providers, create departments, and delete departments. All operations require OWNER role and verify organization membership.
architecture_note: This API follows an actor-based approach: All mutations are under /api/owner/* to clearly indicate who can perform the action. Mutations require OWNER role and verify that the owner is a member of the organization being managed.
core_goals: Enable owners to create provider user accounts and provider records within their organizations.
Enable owners to manage departments within their organizations.
Ensure owners can only manage resources in organizations they belong to.
Enforce organization membership verification for all operations.
Maintain clear actor-based structure for authorization.
key_features: Provider Creation API to create provider with user account (creates user, account, provider record, and adds to organization members).
Provider Record Creation API to create provider record for existing users.
Provider Deletion API to delete providers and clean up organization memberships.
Department Creation API to create departments within owner's organization.
Department Deletion API to delete departments and manage organization state.
Organization membership verification for all operations.
Automatic organization enable/disable logic based on departments and providers.
Secure endpoints protected by Better Auth session authentication and owner role authorization.
user_flow_summary: Owner logs in and authenticates using Better Auth session cookies.
Owner creates departments for their organization via POST /api/owner/departments.
Owner creates providers with user accounts via POST /api/owner/providers/create-user (creates user, account, provider, and adds to org members).
Owner creates provider records for existing users via POST /api/owner/providers.
System verifies organization membership for all operations.
System automatically enables/disables organizations based on resources.
validation_criteria: All API endpoints enforce authentication and return appropriate 401/403 responses for unauthorized access.
Owner mutation endpoints verify that the owner is a member of the organization being managed.
Provider creation validates required fields (name, email, organizationId, departmentId).
Provider creation prevents duplicate email addresses.
Department creation validates required fields (name, organizationId).
Department creation verifies department belongs to owner's organization.
Provider deletion removes provider record and cleans up organization membership.
Department deletion removes department and checks if organization should be disabled.
All mutations verify organization membership before allowing operations.

authentication.note: Use session cookies, NOT bearer tokens. Authenticate via POST /api/auth/sign-in/email first.

test_scenarios: Create provider with user account (complete flow: user, account, provider, member)
Create provider validation (missing required fields, duplicate email, invalid organization/department)
Create provider record for existing user
Delete provider (removes provider and organization membership)
Delete provider validation (not found, not owner's organization)
Create department validation (missing required fields, invalid organization, not owner's organization)
Delete department (removes department, checks organization state)
Delete department validation (not found, not owner's organization)
Authorization checks (owner can only manage own organization resources)

actor_based_mutations.note: All endpoints are actor-based (/api/owner/*) - clearly indicate who can perform the action

endpoints.description: Creates a new provider with a user account. Creates user, account, provider record, and adds provider to organization members. Sends welcome email with temporary password. Returns provider details and temporary password.

authorization.membership_check: Owner must be a member of the specified organization

organizationId.description: ID of the organization (owner must be a member)

departmentId.description: ID of the department (must belong to the organization)

validation_rules: name, email, organizationId, and departmentId are required
Email must be a valid email format
Email must be unique (user with email must not exist)
Owner must be a member of the specified organization
Department must belong to the specified organization

success_response.description: Provider created successfully with user account

scenarios: Missing required fields (name, email, organizationId, or departmentId)
Owner is not a member of the specified organization

body.error: You are not a member of this organization

scenarios: Department not found or does not belong to the organization

body.error: Department not found or does not belong to this organization

endpoints.description: Creates a provider record for an existing user. Used when the user already exists and needs to be associated with a department as a provider. Attempts to enable organization if conditions are met.

authorization.membership_check: Owner must be a member of the organization

scenarios: Owner is not a member of the organization

body.error: You are not a member of this organization

endpoints.description: Deletes a provider record and removes the provider from organization members if they are a member. Checks if organization should be disabled after deletion.

authorization.membership_check: Owner must be a member of the organization that owns the provider

validation_rules: Owner must be a member of the provider's organization

scenarios: Owner is not a member of the provider's organization

body.error: You are not a member of this organization

endpoints.description: Creates a new department within the owner's organization. Verifies owner is a member of the organization. Attempts to enable organization if conditions are met.

authorization.membership_check: Owner must be a member of the specified organization

organizationId.description: ID of the organization (owner must be a member)

validation_rules: Owner must be a member of the specified organization

scenarios: Owner is not a member of the specified organization

body.error: You are not a member of this organization

endpoints.description: Deletes a department. Verifies owner is a member of the department's organization. Checks if organization should be disabled after deletion.

authorization.membership_check: Owner must be a member of the department's organization

validation_rules: Owner must be a member of the department's organization

scenarios: Owner is not a member of the department's organization

body.error: You are not a member of this organization

authentication.description: This application uses Better Auth with session-based authentication (cookies), NOT bearer tokens. Authentication is done via POST request to /api/auth/sign-in/email with email and password. The response includes session cookies in Set-Cookie headers. Use requests.Session() to automatically manage cookies. Include session cookies in all subsequent requests. DO NOT use Authorization: Bearer headers.

credentials.name: John Smith
credentials.note: This user has OWNER role and can test all owner mutation endpoints. The owner must be a member of an organization to test the endpoints effectively.

authentication.example: session = requests.Session(); session.post('http://localhost:3000/api/auth/sign-in/email', json={'email': 'elystrade@gmail.com', 'password': 'bel2000BELLE$'}); response = session.post('http://localhost:3000/api/owner/departments', json={'name': 'Test Department', 'organizationId': '<orgId>'})

test_instructions.note: TestSprite should generate SELF-CONTAINED tests (no external imports like auth_helper). All authentication code must be INLINE within each test file. TestSprite should generate tests that:
requirements: Generate SELF-CONTAINED test files with ALL authentication code INLINE - do NOT import from external modules like auth_helper
Use requests.Session() for session-based authentication (Better Auth cookies), NOT bearer tokens
Authenticate by calling POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with email: elystrade@gmail.com, password: bel2000BELLE$
Fetch owner's organizations by calling GET /api/organizations/my-organizations after authentication
Extract organization ID from the response (use the first enabled organization, or first organization if none enabled)
Use the fetched organization ID for all test operations
Verify organization membership checks - owners can only manage their own organization resources
Test validation rules (required fields, duplicate emails, invalid organizations/departments)
Test authorization - unauthorized users cannot access owner mutation endpoints
Use the provided owner credentials for mutation tests
Test provider creation with user account (complete flow)
Test provider record creation for existing users
test_instructions.authentication_example: session = requests.Session(); auth_resp = session.post('http://localhost:3000/api/auth/sign-in/email', json={'email': 'elystrade@gmail.com', 'password': 'bel2000BELLE$'}, timeout=30); assert auth_resp.status_code == 200; orgs_resp = session.get('http://localhost:3000/api/organizations/my-organizations', timeout=30); orgs = orgs_resp.json(); organization_id = next((org['id'] for org in orgs if org.get('enabled')), orgs[0]['id'] if orgs else None)
